title: MachineElements Object
description: Collection of machine elements. These elements contain the properties that define the machine.

methods.name: addElement
methods.description: Add a new machine element to the machine.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: countByType
methods.description: Number of elements of specified type.

methods.name: createMachineElementInput
methods.description: Create a new MachineElementInput object for the specified type. This is intedned to be used to create/add new machine elements.

methods.name: defaultItemByType
methods.description: Returns the default item of the given type. In most cases this will be the element with an element ID of "default".

methods.name: item
methods.description: Get the element at a particular index in the collection.

methods.name: itemById
methods.description: Gets an element of a specific type by ID.

methods.name: itemsByType
methods.description: Gets the element of specified type.

properties.name: count
properties.description: Total number of elements in collection.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

related_samples.name: Additive Manufacturing MJF API Sample
related_samples.description:
Demonstrates how to automate the creation of an additive MJF manufacturing setup and arrange components within the build volume of a 3D printer.

To run the sample script, have a design with one or more components open in Fusion’s DESIGN workspace. This script will switch the UI from the DESIGN workspace to the MANUFACTURE workspace, create a new Manufacturing Model, and create an Additive Arrange using that manufacturing model as an input.

The setup will select an MJF 3D printer from Fusion’s machine library and a print setting from the print setting library. All components in the Manufacturing model will be automatically arranged within the build volume of the selected MJF machine.

addElement.title: MachineElements.addElement Method
addElement.description: Add a new machine element to the machine.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: A specialization of MachineElementInput class that contains the properties required to create a new machine element.

classType.title: MachineElements.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

countByType.title: MachineElements.countByType Method
countByType.description: Number of elements of specified type.
countByType.return_description: Returns the number of elements of the requested type. Returns zero if no elements match the specified type ID.

parameters.name: typeId
parameters.description: Element typeId to filter. See staticTypeId for the desired element type.

createMachineElementInput.title: MachineElements.createMachineElementInput Method
createMachineElementInput.description: Create a new MachineElementInput object for the specified type. This is intedned to be used to create/add new machine elements.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: The type of machine element to create the input for

defaultItemByType.title: MachineElements.defaultItemByType Method
defaultItemByType.description: Returns the default item of the given type. In most cases this will be the element with an element ID of "default".
defaultItemByType.return_description: Returns the specified Element or null if no matching type ID is found.

parameters.name: typeId
parameters.description: Element typeId to get the default for. See staticTypeId for the desired element type.

item.title: MachineElements.item Method
item.description: Get the element at a particular index in the collection.
item.return_description: Returns the element at the given index.

itemById.title: MachineElements.itemById Method
itemById.description: Gets an element of a specific type by ID.
itemById.return_description: Returns an element of the desired type with the specified ID or null in the case where no match is found.

parameters.name: typeId
parameters.description: Element typeId to filter. See staticTypeId for the desired element type.

itemsByType.title: MachineElements.itemsByType Method
itemsByType.description: Gets the element of specified type.
itemsByType.return_description: Returns a list of elements filtered to the specified type or an empty array if there is no match with the specified typeId.

parameters.name: typeId
parameters.description: Element typeId to filter. See staticTypeId for the desired element type.

related_samples.name: Additive Manufacturing MJF API Sample
related_samples.description:
Demonstrates how to automate the creation of an additive MJF manufacturing setup and arrange components within the build volume of a 3D printer.

To run the sample script, have a design with one or more components open in Fusion’s DESIGN workspace. This script will switch the UI from the DESIGN workspace to the MANUFACTURE workspace, create a new Manufacturing Model, and create an Additive Arrange using that manufacturing model as an input.

The setup will select an MJF 3D printer from Fusion’s machine library and a print setting from the print setting library. All components in the Manufacturing model will be automatically arranged within the build volume of the selected MJF machine.
